{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resources Officer",
  "description": "BlueChip Technologies is a leading business application firm focused exclusively on assisting organizations in planning, designing, implementing and operating business application solutions and strategies that are central to creating and maintaining a competitive business advantage.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Human Resources Officer&nbsp;

Job Location: Lagos

Description


	As part of our continued growth, we are seeking a proactive and experienced Human Resources Officer to join our Human Resources team.
	The successful candidate will support the execution of core HR functions, including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, policy implementation, learning and development, and HR administration, while ensuring compliance with organizational policies and applicable labour laws.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate end-to-end recruitment activities, including job postings, candidate screening, interview scheduling, and onboarding of new employees.
	Maintain accurate employee records and ensure all HR documentation is up to date and compliant.
	Administer employee onboarding, orientation, and exit processes.
	Support the implementation of performance management initiatives, including appraisals, probation reviews, and performance improvement plans.
	Serve as the first point of contact for employee queries and provide guidance on HR policies, procedures, and workplace matters.
	Coordinate learning and development programs, including training logistics, records, and evaluations.
	Prepare accurate learning and development reports.
	Ensure compliance with labour regulations, company policies, and HR best practices.
	Support payroll administration by ensuring timely and accurate submission of employee data and payroll-related changes.
	Collaborate with departmental managers to address workforce planning and staffing requirements.
	Participate in HR projects and continuous process improvement initiatives.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, Psychology, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 2&ndash;4 years of professional experience in Human Resources.
	Hands-on experience across key HR functions, including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, onboarding, and HR administration.
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian Labour Law and HR best practices.
	Experience using HR Information Systems (HRIS) and Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel.
	Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	High level of professionalism, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Professional HR certifications (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, CIPD, or equivalent) will be an added advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement abilities.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Strong organizational, planning, and time management skills.
	Analytical thinking and problem-solving capability.
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work independently and collaboratively within a team.
	Proactive, detail-oriented, and results-driven.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.

Human Resources Officer Job at BlueChip Technologies




        
    

        
        BlueChip Technologies is a leading business application firm focused exclusively on assisting organizations in planning, designing, implementing and operating business application solutions and strategies that are central to creating and maintaining a competitive business advantage.

We are  recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resources Officer 

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

            
    




	As part of our continued growth, we are seeking a proactive and experienced Human Resources Officer to join our Human Resources team.
	The successful candidate will support the execution of core HR functions, including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, policy implementation, learning and development, and HR administration, while ensuring compliance with organizational policies and applicable labour laws.


Key Responsibilities


	Coordinate end-to-end recruitment activities, including job postings, candidate screening, interview scheduling, and onboarding of new employees.
	Maintain accurate employee records and ensure all HR documentation is up to date and compliant.
	Administer employee onboarding, orientation, and exit processes.
	Support the implementation of performance management initiatives, including appraisals, probation reviews, and performance improvement plans.
	Serve as the first point of contact for employee queries and provide guidance on HR policies, procedures, and workplace matters.
	Coordinate learning and development programs, including training logistics, records, and evaluations.
	Prepare accurate learning and development reports.
	Ensure compliance with labour regulations, company policies, and HR best practices.
	Support payroll administration by ensuring timely and accurate submission of employee data and payroll-related changes.
	Collaborate with departmental managers to address workforce planning and staffing requirements.
	Participate in HR projects and continuous process improvement initiatives.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Industrial Relations, Psychology, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 2–4 years of professional experience in Human Resources.
	Hands-on experience across key HR functions, including recruitment, employee relations, performance management, onboarding, and HR administration.
	Sound knowledge of Nigerian Labour Law and HR best practices.
	Experience using HR Information Systems (HRIS) and Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel.
	Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	High level of professionalism, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Professional HR certifications (CIPM, SHRM, HRCI, CIPD, or equivalent) will be an added advantage.


Key Competencies:


	Strong interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement abilities.
	High level of integrity and confidentiality.
	Strong organizational, planning, and time management skills.
	Analytical thinking and problem-solving capability.
	Ability to  work independently and collaboratively within a team.
	Proactive, detail-oriented, and results-driven.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word, and PowerPoint.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Manager",
  "description": "Fadac Resources and Services Limited - From recruitment to workforce strategy, we help you build teams that grow with your vision. Let&rsquo;s power your next phase of growth through people.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

GeographicReference

&nbsp;

Job Description


	We&rsquo;re looking for a Sales Manager to drive growth, lead a sales team, and expand our client&#39;sfootprint across Nigeria.
	This is a hands-on leadership role for a proven sales professional who understands enterprise IT solutions and can translate technical capabilities into commercial wins.


Key Responsibilities


	Own and deliver quarterly and annual revenue targets.
	Build and lead a high-performing sales team (Sales Executives, SDRs, Pre-Sales).
	Develop and execute go-to-market strategies for Snapnet&rsquo;s solutions.
	Manage the end-to-end sales cycle &mdash; from prospecting and proposals to closing deals.
	Grow and maintain strategic client and partner relationships.
	Work closely with Pre-Sales, Delivery, and Marketing teams to ensure client satisfaction.
	Track performance through CRM, forecasts, and key sales metrics.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree required; MBA or professional certifications (e.g., MEDDIC, Challenger) are an advantage.
	7&ndash;10 years of B2B IT solution or consulting sales experience, including at least 3 years in a leadership role.
	Proven success selling Cloud, ERP, Managed Services, or Cybersecurity solutions.
	Strong commercial acumen, presentation, and negotiation skills.
	Experience building and mentoring sales teams.
	Excellent knowledge of the Nigerian enterprise market.


What You&rsquo;ll Bring:


	A growth mindset and the drive to scale a sales organisation from the ground up.
	The ability to balance strategic planning with hands-on execution.
	Energy, discipline, and the confidence to engage C-level stakeholders.


What We Offer


	Competitive base salary + performance-based commission
	Health insurance, pension, and other benefits
	Career growth in a dynamic, technology-driven environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: favour.a@fadacresources.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Sales Manager Job at Fadac Resources and Services Limited




        
    

        
        Fadac Resources and Services Limited - From recruitment to workforce strategy, we help you build teams that grow with your vision. Let’s power your next phase of growth through people.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

GeographicReference

 

Job Description
     

            
    




	We’re looking for a Sales Manager to drive growth, lead a sales team, and expand our client'sfootprint across Nigeria.
	This is a hands-on leadership role for a proven sales professional who understands enterprise IT solutions and can translate technical capabilities into commercial wins.


Key Responsibilities


	Own and deliver quarterly and annual revenue targets.
	Build and lead a high-performing sales team (Sales Executives, SDRs, Pre-Sales).
	Develop and execute go-to-market strategies for Snapnet’s solutions.
	Manage the end-to-end sales cycle — from prospecting and proposals to closing deals.
	Grow and maintain strategic client and partner relationships.
	Work closely with Pre-Sales, Delivery, and Marketing teams to ensure client satisfaction.
	Track performance through CRM, forecasts, and key sales metrics.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree required; MBA or professional certifications (e.g., MEDDIC, Challenger) are an advantage.
	7–10 years of B2B IT solution or consulting sales experience, including at least 3 years in a leadership role.
	Proven success selling Cloud, ERP, Managed Services, or Cybersecurity solutions.
	Strong commercial acumen, presentation, and negotiation skills.
	Experience building and mentoring sales teams.
	Excellent knowledge of the Nigerian enterprise market.


What You’ll Bring:


	A growth mindset and the drive to scale a sales organisation from the ground up.
	The ability to balance strategic planning with hands-on execution.
	Energy, discipline, and the confidence to engage C-level stakeholders.


What We Offer


	Competitive base salary + performance-based commission
	Health insurance, pension, and other benefits
	Career growth in a dynamic, technology-driven environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Installation / Construction Manager",
  "description": "Horkey International Services Limited is a wholly owned Nigerian company, which provides services in Dredging, Corrosion Control and Marine support services. We are duly registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission (C.A.C) as a limited Liability company incorporated on the 21st December 2000 in accordance and compliance with the Companies and Allied Matters Decree 1990 of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Installation / Construction Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Installation / Construction Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, supervising, and managing all construction and installation activities to ensure projects are executed safely, on schedule, within budget, and in accordance with approved engineering specifications, quality standards, and client requirements.
	The role provides leadership to construction personnel and coordinates multidisciplinary teams throughout project execution.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan and oversee all site construction and installation activities.
	Develop construction methodologies and execution plans.
	Coordinate field construction teams, subcontractors, and vendors.
	Ensure&nbsp;&nbsp;work complies with engineering drawings, specifications, and approved procedures.
	Monitor project schedules and ensure timely completion of milestones.
	Implement and enforce HSE policies and safe work practices.
	Coordinate daily construction meetings and toolbox talks.
	Ensure quality control and compliance with applicable standards and codes.
	Review and approve construction work packs and method statements.
	Liaise with engineering, procurement, logistics, QA/QC, HSE, and client representatives.
	Monitor manpower, equipment, and material utilization.
	Identify project risks and implement mitigation measures.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly construction progress reports.
	Manage subcontractor performance and productivity.
	Support pre-commissioning, commissioning, and project handover activities.
	Ensure proper documentation of construction records and as-built information.


Required Skills:
Technical Skills:


	Construction planning and execution
	Project coordination and supervision
	Installation management
	Construction scheduling
	Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC)
	HSE Management
	Risk assessment and mitigation
	Resource planning
	Cost control
	Reading engineering drawings and specifications
	Contract administration
	Knowledge of oil &amp; gas construction standards
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Working knowledge of Primavera P6 and/or Microsoft Project is an advantage


Soft Skills:


	Leadership and team management
	Excellent communication skills
	Decision-making ability
	Problem-solving and analytical skills
	Conflict resolution
	Negotiation skills
	Time management
	Ability to work under pressure
	Organizational and planning skills
	High level of integrity and accountability


Educational Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree (B.Eng./B.Sc.) or Higher National Diploma (HND) in:
	
		Civil Engineering
		Construction Engineering
		Marine Engineering
		Structural Engineering
		Building Technology
	
	
	COREN registration is an added advantage.
	Membership of relevant professional bodies (NSE, COREN, PMI, NIM, etc.) is desirable.


Experience:


	Minimum of 15 years of relevant construction or installation experience.
	At least 5 years in a supervisory or management position.
	Proven experience executing EPC, oil &amp; gas, marine, civil construction, pipeline, dredging, or infrastructure projects.
	Experience managing subcontractors and multidisciplinary construction teams.
	Demonstrated ability to deliver projects within schedule and budget.
	Experience working with international clients or major Nigerian operators is an added advantage.


Other Requirements:


	Physically fit and medically certified for site work.
	Willingness to work at project sites and remote locations.
	Ability to work extended hours when required.
	Valid Nigerian driver&#39;s licence is an advantage.
	Good knowledge of Nigerian construction regulations and HSE requirements.
	Strong understanding of permit-to-work systems and safe systems of work.
	Ability to interpret engineering drawings and construction specifications.
	Good report writing and documentation skills.
	Strong commitment to quality, safety, and continuous improvement.
	Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and multidisciplinary environment.


Salary
N500,000 - N700,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment.hr@horkeyinternational.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Installation / Construction Manager Job at Horkey International Services Limited




        
    

        
        Horkey International Services Limited is a wholly owned Nigerian company, which provides services in Dredging, Corrosion Control and Marine support services. We are duly registered with the Corporate Affairs Commission (C.A.C) as a limited Liability company incorporated on the 21st December 2000 in accordance and compliance with the Companies and Allied Matters Decree 1990 of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Installation / Construction Manager

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	The Installation / Construction Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, supervising, and managing all construction and installation activities to ensure projects are executed safely, on schedule, within budget, and in accordance with approved engineering specifications, quality standards, and client requirements.
	The role provides leadership to construction personnel and coordinates multidisciplinary teams throughout project execution.


Key Responsibilities


	Plan and oversee all site construction and installation activities.
	Develop construction methodologies and execution plans.
	Coordinate field construction teams, subcontractors, and vendors.
	Ensure  work complies with engineering drawings, specifications, and approved procedures.
	Monitor project schedules and ensure timely completion of milestones.
	Implement and enforce HSE policies and safe work practices.
	Coordinate daily construction meetings and toolbox talks.
	Ensure quality control and compliance with applicable standards and codes.
	Review and approve construction work packs and method statements.
	Liaise with engineering, procurement, logistics, QA/QC, HSE, and client representatives.
	Monitor manpower, equipment, and material utilization.
	Identify project risks and implement mitigation measures.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly construction progress reports.
	Manage subcontractor performance and productivity.
	Support pre-commissioning, commissioning, and project handover activities.
	Ensure proper documentation of construction records and as-built information.


Required Skills:
Technical Skills:


	Construction planning and execution
	Project coordination and supervision
	Installation management
	Construction scheduling
	Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC)
	HSE Management
	Risk assessment and mitigation
	Resource planning
	Cost control
	Reading engineering drawings and specifications
	Contract administration
	Knowledge of oil & gas construction standards
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
	Working knowledge of Primavera P6 and/or Microsoft Project is an advantage


Soft Skills:


	Leadership and team management
	Excellent communication skills
	Decision-making ability
	Problem-solving and analytical skills
	Conflict resolution
	Negotiation skills
	Time management
	Ability to work under pressure
	Organizational and planning skills
	High level of integrity and accountability


Educational Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree (B.Eng./B.Sc.) or Higher National Diploma (HND) in:
	
		Civil Engineering
		Construction Engineering
		Marine Engineering
		Structural Engineering
		Building Technology
	
	
	COREN registration is an added advantage.
	Membership of relevant professional bodies (NSE, COREN, PMI, NIM, etc.) is desirable.


Experience:


	Minimum of 15 years of relevant construction or installation experience.
	At least 5 years in a supervisory or management position.
	Proven experience executing EPC, oil & gas, marine, civil construction, pipeline, dredging, or infrastructure projects.
	Experience managing subcontractors and multidisciplinary construction teams.
	Demonstrated ability to deliver projects within schedule and budget.
	Experience working with international clients or major Nigerian operators is an added advantage.


Other Requirements:


	Physically fit and medically certified for site work.
	Willingness to work at project sites and remote locations.
	Ability to work extended hours when required.
	Valid Nigerian driver's licence is an advantage.
	Good knowledge of Nigerian construction regulations and HSE requirements.
	Strong understanding of permit-to-work systems and safe systems of work.
	Ability to interpret engineering drawings and construction specifications.
	Good report writing and documentation skills.
	Strong commitment to quality, safety, and continuous improvement.
	Ability to work effectively in a multicultural and multidisciplinary environment.


Salary
N500,000 - N700,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Administrator - Business Partnering",
  "description": "Sundry Foods is an integrated food services company founded in 2003 and operating in major cities in Nigeria. We are bringing our now trademark great tasting ready-to-eat food and quality services to thousands of people and institutions daily through its chain and network of restaurants, bakeries and other catering facilities.

We are proud to own and operate one of Africa&rsquo;s best restaurant and bakery chains and we pride ourselves on our ability to consistently deliver original food and service solutions whether in workplaces, schools and colleges, hospitals, joining even remote sites and different cultural environments using our team of young seasoned professionals and an entire workforce with a passion to deliver nothing but the best to every customer.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Administrator - Business Partnering

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full-Time

Description


	The Human Resources Administrators will be responsible for implementing Human Resources management best practices within assigned business divisions and regions.


Responsibilities
The role holder will:


	Be the first point of contact to receive and resolve all people-related enquiries or complaints within assigned business division or region.
	Manage recruitment, selection, and on-boarding of new hires within the region to ensure appropriate staffing levels.
	Partner with business unit managers to build their leadership capability in the management of their staff and compliance with HR Policies.
	Facilitate regular refresher trainings (and ensure implementation of on-the-job training) to ensure consistency in employees&rsquo; skills and attitude at work.
	Coordinate Staff Engagement programs and Meetings to identify pertinent employee and work-place issues, provide pragmatic solutions and report outcomes


Requirements


	A Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree with a minimum of second class lower and 4 years of experience in Human Resources Management functions in a fast-paced work environment
	Candidate with experience in Recruitment and training preferred.
	Candidate with HR Certification (CIPM, CIPD, etc) will have an added advantage
	Candidate with process mindset and able to re-engineer processes is desirable.


Required Skill:


	Microsoft Office Suite skills (Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Outlook); Excellent verbal and communication skills; presentation skills; facilitation skills

HR Administrator - Business Partnering Job at Sundry Foods




        
    

        
        Sundry Foods is an integrated food services company founded in 2003 and operating in major cities in Nigeria. We are bringing our now trademark great tasting ready-to-eat food and quality services to thousands of people and institutions daily through its chain and network of restaurants, bakeries and other catering facilities.

We are proud to own and operate one of Africa’s best restaurant and bakery chains and we pride ourselves on our ability to consistently deliver original food and service solutions whether in workplaces, schools and colleges, hospitals, joining even remote sites and different cultural environments using our team of young seasoned professionals and an entire workforce with a passion to deliver nothing but the best to every customer.
     

            
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Administrator - Business Partnering

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full-Time

Description


	The Human Resources Administrators will be responsible for implementing Human Resources management best practices within assigned business divisions and regions.


Responsibilities
The role holder will:


	Be the first point of contact to receive and resolve all people-related enquiries or complaints within assigned business division or region.
	Manage recruitment, selection, and on-boarding of new hires within the region to ensure appropriate staffing levels.
	Partner with business unit managers to build their leadership capability in the management of their staff and compliance with HR Policies.
	Facilitate regular refresher trainings (and ensure implementation of on-the-job training) to ensure consistency in employees’ skills and attitude at work.
	Coordinate Staff Engagement programs and Meetings to identify pertinent employee and work-place issues, provide pragmatic solutions and report outcomes


Requirements


	A Bachelor’s Degree with a minimum of second class lower and 4 years of experience in Human Resources Management functions in a fast-paced work environment
	Candidate with experience in Recruitment and training preferred.
	Candidate with HR Certification (CIPM, CIPD, etc) will have an added advantage
	Candidate with process mindset and able to re-engineer processes is desirable.


Required Skill:


	Microsoft Office Suite skills (Word, Excel, Powerpoint, Outlook); Excellent verbal and communication skills; presentation skills; facilitation skills
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Business Development Manager (Real Estate) Job at Dicalo Consulting Group




        
    

        
        Dicalo Consulting Group is a management consulting firm specializing in a range of services, including business management and setup, accounting services, business registration, business consultancy, international tax advisory, and business plan preparation. At Dicalo Consulting, our business practices are guided by a set of core values that shape our approach to conducting business.
     

            
    



We are a group of dedicated professionals who prioritize honesty and unwavering transparency in our pricing and services. Our commitment is deeply rooted in strong ethical and moral principles, and we take great pride in delivering exceptional services. Even in the face of occasional challenges in the Tax, Accountancy and recruitment process, we maintain our steadfast commitment to providing services that not only add value but also bring gratification, consistently surpassing our clients’ expectations.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position:  Business Development Manager (Real Estate)
Job Location: Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director / Head of Business Development

Job Summary


	The Business Development Manager will be responsible for driving revenue growth by identifying new business opportunities, building strategic partnerships, expanding the company’s client base, and leading business development initiatives.
	The successful candidate will also support the sales team in achieving organizational revenue goals while strengthening the company’s market presence.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute business development strategies to increase revenue.
	Identify new business opportunities, strategic partnerships, and investment prospects.
	Generate high-value leads and convert them into profitable business opportunities.
	Build and maintain relationships with corporate clients, investors, and key stakeholders.
	Supervise and support the Sales Executives to achieve sales targets.
	Conduct market research to identify emerging trends and customer needs.
	Lead client meetings, negotiations, and contract discussions.
	Coordinate property presentations and investment pitches.
	Monitor sales performance and prepare business development reports.
	Represent the company at industry events, exhibitions, and networking forums.
	Collaborate with marketing and management teams on promotional campaigns.
	Ensure exceptional customer experience throughout the client journey.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Marketing, Estate Management, or a related discipline.
	2–4 years of experience in business development or sales, preferably within the real estate industry.
	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Proven ability to develop and close high-value business opportunities.
	Strong understanding of the Lagos real estate market.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Highly organized, strategic, and target-oriented.
	Willingness to travel for client meetings, site inspections, and business development activities.
	Applicants must reside within the Lekki, Ajah, Sangotedo, Abraham Adesanya, or nearby environs.


Work Schedule:


	Monday – Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM
	Saturdays: As required for client inspections and business activities


Salary & Benefits


	N200,000 – N300,000 Monthly
	HMO Coverage, Attractive Commission Structure, Career Growth Opportunities


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
